ENGAA-2016-M1-001: Find the complete set of solutions to $-8<6-\dfrac{x}{2}$.
ENGAA-2016-PHY-001: A nuclide $^{214}_{82}\text{Pb}$ changes by radioactive decay into the nuclide $^{210}_{82}\text{Pb}$. Which combination of emissions produces this change?
ENGAA-2016-M1-002: Which one of the following is a simplification of $(\sqrt3-\sqrt2)^2$?
ENGAA-2016-PHY-002: The graph shown of quantity y against quantity x represents the motion of a body. [DIAGRAM: straight line through origin passing through point (2.0, 10), axes in SI units, g = 10 N/kg]. Which two of the following could the graph represent? (1) kinetic energy against velocity for an object of mass 10 kg undergoing free-fall; (2) potential energy against height for an object of mass 20 kg being lifted by a constant external force; (3) velocity against time for an object of mass 20 kg being accelerated by a resultant force of 100 N; (4) work done by an external force of 5 N against distance moved for an object of mass 12 kg being moved at constant speed by (and in the direction of) the external force.
ENGAA-2016-M1-003: The ratio of $Q:R$ is $5:2$ and the ratio of $R:S$ is $3:10$. Which one of the following gives the ratio $Q:S$ in its simplest form?
ENGAA-2016-PHY-003: A uranium-235 nucleus can undergo fission to produce two smaller nuclei. [DIAGRAM: three candidate fission reaction diagrams]. Diagram 1: a proton plus $^{235}_{92}\text{U}$ produces $^{89}_{36}\text{Kr}$, $^{144}_{56}\text{Ba}$, and 2 neutrons. Diagram 2: a neutron plus $^{235}_{92}\text{U}$ produces $^{96}_{38}\text{Sr}$, $^{137}_{54}\text{Xe}$, and 3 neutrons. Diagram 3: a neutron plus $^{235}_{92}\text{U}$ produces $^{145}_{57}\text{La}$, $^{87}_{35}\text{Br}$, and 3 neutrons. Which of the diagrams, if any, could represent this process?
ENGAA-2016-M1-004: The mean age of the twenty members of a running club is exactly 28. The mean age increases by exactly 2 years when two new members join. What is the mean age of the two new members?
ENGAA-2016-PHY-004: A circuit consists of a 5.0 Ω resistor and a variable resistor connected in series with a 24 V battery. The variable resistor has a minimum resistance of 3.0 Ω and a maximum resistance of 15 Ω. The battery and the connecting wires have negligible resistance. What is the maximum power dissipated in the 5.0 Ω resistor?
ENGAA-2016-M1-005: A medical scanner is bought for £15 000. The value of the scanner depreciates by 20% every year. By how much has the scanner reduced in value after 2 years?
ENGAA-2016-PHY-005: The total power $P$ radiated by a star is given by $P = kR^2T^4$ where $R$ is the radius of the star, $T$ is its surface temperature and $k$ is a constant. The power currently radiated by the Sun is $4.0\times10^{26}$ W. Towards the end of the Sun's life its radius will increase by a factor of a hundred and its surface temperature will decrease by a factor of two. What will be the power radiated by the Sun when these changes have occurred?
ENGAA-2016-M1-006: The point A is 4 km due East of the point B. The bearing of the point C from A is $330°$ and the bearing of C from B is $060°$. Find the distance BC.
ENGAA-2016-PHY-006: A transverse wave travelling through a medium has a frequency of 5.0 Hz, a wavelength of 4.0 cm and an amplitude of 3.0 cm. What is the total distance travelled by a particle of the medium in one minute?
ENGAA-2016-M1-007: The quantities $x$ and $y$ are positive. $x$ is inversely proportional to the square root of $y$. When $x=8$, $y=9$. What is the value of $y$ when $x=6$?
ENGAA-2016-PHY-007: A motor is used to lift a mass of 5.0 kg using a pulley system. [DIAGRAM: a fixed pulley attached to the roof by a coupling; a rope passes over the pulley with the mass on one side and the motor pulling the other side]. The motor needs to cause the mass to accelerate upwards at 0.80 m s$^{-2}$. What is the minimum tension force that the coupling must be able to withstand without breaking? (g = 10 N kg$^{-1}$; frictionless, massless pulley; massless, inextensible rope.)
ENGAA-2016-M1-008: In a trapezium PQRS, the parallel sides are PQ and RS. $PQ=(x-1)$ cm, $RS=(x+5)$ cm and the vertical height $QR=x$ cm. The area of the trapezium is 120 cm². What is the length of RS?
ENGAA-2016-PHY-008: A heater is connected in series with a resistor and a 6.0 V battery. The total resistance of the circuit is 15 Ω. In 3.0 minutes, 180 J of electrical energy is transferred into other forms in the heater. How much charge flows through the heater in the 3.0 minutes and what is the voltage across the heater?
ENGAA-2016-M1-009: Make $b$ the subject of the formula: $a=\dfrac{b^2+2}{3b^2-1}$
ENGAA-2016-PHY-009: A cubic block has a hole through it with a square cross-section. [DIAGRAM: cube of side 10 cm with a 5.0 cm square hole through it]. The weight of the block is 30 N. What is the density of the material from which the block is made? (g = 10 N kg$^{-1}$.)
ENGAA-2016-M1-010: A thin rectangular sheet of metal 10 m by 5 m is made into an open ended cylinder by joining the edges PS and QR. The height of the cylinder is 10 m. What is the volume, in cubic metres, enclosed by this cylinder?
ENGAA-2016-PHY-010: The diagram shows four solid steel balls P, Q, R and S of identical size. Balls P and R have shiny surfaces; balls Q and S have dull surfaces. Balls P and Q are in a room at 20 °C, both currently at 30 °C. Balls R and S are in a room at 40 °C, both currently at 40 °C. Which two balls lose thermal energy by convection, and which ball emits thermal radiation at the greatest rate?
ENGAA-2016-M1-011: Which one of the following is a simplification of $4+\dfrac{4-x^2}{x^2-2x}$?
ENGAA-2016-PHY-011: The diagram shows the velocity-time graph for an object travelling in a straight line over a period of 30 s. [DIAGRAM: velocity-time graph, velocity axis from -2.0 to 8.0 m/s, time axis 0 to 30 s in segments of 10 s]. What total distance did the object travel in the 30 s, how far from its starting position was it at the end of the 30 s, and what was its average speed over the 30 s?
ENGAA-2016-M1-012: During summer activities week 120 students each chose one activity from swimming, archery, and tennis. 46 of the students were girls (so 74 were boys). 36 of the students chose tennis, and $\tfrac{2}{3}$ of these were boys; 25 girls chose swimming, and 27 students chose archery. A boy is picked at random. What is the probability that he chose swimming?
ENGAA-2016-M1-013: Bronze is a mixture of tin and copper. A particular sample of bronze contains 10% tin by volume (10% of the total volume is tin, 90% is copper). What percentage of the mass of the sample is tin? (Density of tin $=Y$ and density of copper $=X$.)
ENGAA-2016-M1-014: Which one of the following expressions is equivalent to $\dfrac{9^{2n+1}\times3^{4-3n}}{27^{2-n}}$?
ENGAA-2016-PHY-012: When a stationary uranium-238 nucleus decays by alpha emission it forms a nucleus of thorium-234. The total kinetic energy produced by the decay is $E$. What is the kinetic energy of the alpha particle?
ENGAA-2016-M1-015: In the diagram, PQRS is part of a regular polygon with $n$ sides. The side PQ is extended to T such that PQT is a straight line. The length of RQ is the same as the length of RT. Find an equation for $n$ in terms of $x$, where $x$ is the size of angle $\angle QRT$ in degrees.
ENGAA-2016-PHY-013: A student carries out an experiment to measure the speed of sound. A loudspeaker that emits sound in all directions is placed between two buildings that are 128 m apart. The student and loudspeaker are 48 m from one of the buildings (and thus 80 m from the other). The loudspeaker emits regular clicks; each click results in two echoes, one from each building. The rate of clicks is gradually increased from zero until each echo coincides with a new click being emitted. What is the frequency of emission of clicks when this happens? (Speed of sound in air = 320 m s$^{-1}$.)
ENGAA-2016-M2-001: When $x=2$ is substituted in the expression $x^3+px^2+qx+p^2$ the result is 0. When $x=1$ is substituted into the same expression, the result is $-3.5$. Find all possible value(s) of $p$.
ENGAA-2016-PHY-014: A parachutist is falling at terminal speed with his parachute open. Separate force diagrams show the vertical forces on the parachute and on the parachutist: air resistance on parachute (L), weight of parachute (M), force from parachutist on parachute (N), force from parachute on parachutist (P), air resistance on parachutist (Q), weight of parachutist (R). Consider: Equation 1: $L=M+N$; Equation 2: $R=P+Q$; Equation 3: $L=Q$; Equation 4: $N=P$; Equation 5: $L+R=Q+M$. Which of these equations, if any, is/are the direct result of applying Newton's Third Law to this situation?
ENGAA-2016-M2-002: A square PQRS is drawn above the x-axis with the side PQ on the x-axis. P is the point $(-5,0)$ and Q is the point $(1,0)$. A circle is drawn inside the square with diameter equal in length to the side of the square. Which one of the following is an equation of the circle?
ENGAA-2016-PHY-015: A crate has a total mass of 800 kg, including its contents. A helicopter of mass 4200 kg is carrying the crate using a light inextensible rope. The helicopter and crate are accelerating upwards at 2.0 m s$^{-2}$. What is the tension in the rope? (g = 10 N kg$^{-1}$; air resistance can be ignored.)
ENGAA-2016-M2-003: The first term of a convergent geometric series is 8. The fifth term is 2. The sixth term is real and positive. What is the sum to infinity of this series? (Sum to infinity $=\dfrac{a}{1-r}$.)
ENGAA-2016-PHY-016: A shopper pushes a supermarket trolley a distance of 15 m in a straight line across a level, horizontal surface. The shopper applies a constant force of 50 N at an angle of 37° below the horizontal. The total weight of the trolley and its contents is 350 N. What is the magnitude of the total vertical force that the surface exerts on the trolley and how much work is done by the pushing force? (sin 37° = 0.60; cos 37° = 0.80.)
ENGAA-2016-M2-004: Tangents are drawn from a point P to a circle of radius 10 cm. The centre of the circle is C and the distance PC is 20 cm. Which one of the following is an expression for the shaded area (bounded by the two tangents and the circle) in square centimetres?
ENGAA-2016-PHY-017: A plank of non-uniform density with mass 15 kg is used to make a see-saw, pivoted at its centre. A boy of mass 35 kg sits with his centre of gravity 1.20 m from the pivot on one side. The see-saw balances when a woman of mass 60 kg sits with her centre of gravity 0.80 m from the pivot on the other side. Where is the centre of gravity of the plank and what is the magnitude of the force between the pivot and the plank? (g = 10 N kg$^{-1}$.)
ENGAA-2016-M2-005: Given that $7\cos\theta-3\tan\theta\sin\theta=1$, which one of the following is true?
ENGAA-2016-PHY-018: A car of mass 200 kg on a fairground ride travels at a speed of 5.0 m s$^{-1}$ at point X, at height 8.0 m above the ground. The car moves down a sloping track without any energy input to point Y, at height 2.0 m above the ground, where its speed is 9.0 m s$^{-1}$. How much energy is transferred in overcoming resistive forces as the car travels from X to Y? (g = 10 N kg$^{-1}$.)
ENGAA-2016-M2-006: The complete set of values of $a$ for which the equation $3x^2=(a+2)x-3$ has two real distinct roots is:
ENGAA-2016-PHY-019: The diagram shows a uniform, solid, heavy cube with side $d$, resting with one edge in contact with a level table. A horizontal force P acts on another edge of the cube at 30° [DIAGRAM: cube with horizontal force P applied near the top edge, contact edge at bottom, 30° angle indicated], and the cube is stationary. Consider: (1) It is possible that there is no frictional force between the cube and the table. (2) There must be a frictional force acting to the left between the cube and the table. (3) There must be a frictional force acting to the right between the cube and the table. (4) Force P has a clockwise moment about the edge in contact with the table equal to $P\times d$. Which of the statements is/are correct?
ENGAA-2016-M2-007: The straight line with equation $y=mx+3$, where $m>0$, $m\neq1$, is perpendicular to the line with equation $y=px+2$. The lines cut the x-axis at points L and M respectively. The length of LM is 5 units. What is the value of $m+p$ given that $m>1$?
ENGAA-2016-PHY-020: The diagram shows two identical blocks, each of mass $m$, in two different arrangements, with heights $a$ and $b$ indicated. [DIAGRAM: arrangement 1 and arrangement 2 showing blocks stacked/positioned differently with height labels a and b]. Which expression gives (gravitational PE of arrangement 2) $-$ (gravitational PE of arrangement 1)? (g is the gravitational field strength.)
ENGAA-2016-M2-008: $f(x)=x^3-a^2x$ where $a$ is a positive constant. Find the complete set of values of $x$ for which $f(x)$ is an increasing function.
ENGAA-2016-PHY-021: An object is fired vertically upwards from the ground at time $t=0$ s in still air at a speed of 8.0 m s$^{-1}$. On the way up, what is the height of the object above the ground when it has a speed of 2.0 m s$^{-1}$, and at what time does it reach this height on the way down? (g = 10 N kg$^{-1}$; air resistance ignored.)
ENGAA-2016-M2-009: The curve $y=x^2$ is translated by the vector $\begin{pmatrix}4\\3\end{pmatrix}$ and then reflected in the line $y=-1$. Which one of the following is an equation of the resulting curve?
ENGAA-2016-PHY-022: A ball P, of mass 4.0 kg, moves to the right at 10 m s$^{-1}$ directly towards a stationary ball Q, of mass 2.0 kg. The balls collide but do not join together. Immediately after the collision ball Q moves at 10 m s$^{-1}$ to the right. What is the velocity of ball P immediately after the collision, and how much kinetic energy in total is lost during the collision?
ENGAA-2016-M2-010: The complete set of values of $x$ for which $4x^4-17x^2+4>0$ is:
ENGAA-2016-PHY-023: A point object of mass 2.0 kg is at rest on a level, horizontal surface. The coefficient of friction between the object and the surface is 0.25. Two horizontal forces at right-angles to each other, with magnitudes 9.0 N and 12.0 N, are applied simultaneously to the object. What is the magnitude of the acceleration of the object as it begins to move? (g = 10 N kg$^{-1}$.)
ENGAA-2016-M2-011: A cursor starts at the point $(0,10)$ and moves parallel to the x-axis in the negative direction. What is the minimum distance parallel to the y-axis between the cursor and the graph of $y=4x^3-12x^2-36x-15$?
ENGAA-2016-PHY-024: An object of mass 20 kg is pulled up a rough plane inclined at 30° to the horizontal by a light, inextensible cable attached via a frictionless pulley to a freely-falling 30 kg mass [DIAGRAM: 30 kg mass hanging vertically over a pulley, connected via cable to a 20 kg mass on a 30° incline]. The acceleration of the object along the plane is 2.5 m s$^{-2}$. What is the frictional force between the object and the plane? (g = 10 N kg$^{-1}$; sin30°=0.5, cos30°=$\frac{\sqrt3}{2}$.)
ENGAA-2016-M2-012: What is the area enclosed by the line $x=7$ and the curve $x=3(y-1)^2+4$?
ENGAA-2016-PHY-025: A spacecraft of initial total mass 4000 kg is travelling relative to the Earth at a constant speed of 7425 m s$^{-1}$. It ejects some fuel backwards in a sudden burst at a speed relative to the spacecraft of 1425 m s$^{-1}$. As a result, the speed of the spacecraft immediately after the fuel is ejected increases to 7500 m s$^{-1}$. What is the mass of fuel ejected?
ENGAA-2016-M2-013: A curve has equation $y=3x^4-4x^3-12x^2+20$. What is the complete set of values of the constant $k$ for which the equation $3x^4-4x^3-12x^2+20=k$ has exactly four distinct real roots?
ENGAA-2016-PHY-026: An object of weight 40 N hangs from the end of a light inextensible string of length 0.35 m, attached to the ceiling. A constant horizontal force of 30 N is applied to the object, causing it to move to a new equilibrium position with the string no longer vertical. By how much has the gravitational potential energy of the object increased as a result of its change of position?
